workflow.name: Sales follow-up email from call notes
workflow.why_this_workflow: Sales follow-up emails are repeated, economically valuable, easy to score, low-risk, and common across many organizations.
workflow.real_work_unit: Draft one buyer-specific follow-up email from call notes.
workflow.note: The proof uses deterministic local agents and realistic sample call notes so it can run in GitHub Actions without API keys or private customer data.

human_only_baseline.name: Human-only baseline
human_only_baseline.assumption: Fully loaded human labor at $75/hour; 12 minutes to draft, revise, and send one follow-up.

case_results.output_preview:
Hi Ari,

Thanks again for the conversation about NovaWorks.

It sounds like there is a strong opportunity to improve the workflow. I also wanted to recap that our platform can help teams coordinate follow-ups, reduce manual work, and keep stakeholders aligned across the customer journey. I attached a deck with the numbers we discussed.

Let me know what you think and we can keep things moving.

Best,
Agent SkillOS

instructions: Put the agreed next step in the first three lines.
Mention the buyer's specific pain point instead of generic value language.
End with one concrete yes/no call to action.
Keep the email under 120 words unless the user asks for detail.
Do not invent commitments, dates, metrics, or attachments that are not present in the notes.
